title: A Remarkable Teacher Meets Her Student
story: A young teacher named Anne Sullivan arrived at a house in Alabama. She was there to meet Helen Keller, a 6-year-old girl who couldn't see or hear. It was March 3, 1887, the start of an amazing friendship. Anne had a special way to teach Helen using hand signals. She spelled words into Helen's palm, helping her understand the world around her. This method would change Helen's life forever.

follow_up_question: How old was Helen Keller when Anne Sullivan began teaching her?

title: Apollo 9: Rehearsal for Moon Landing
story: Blast off! On March 3, 1969, Apollo 9 roared into the sky, kicking off a crucial mission. For ten days, astronauts circled Earth, testing the lunar module that would later land on the moon. title: Taking Flight: The Birth of NACA
story: Soaring dreams of flight took a giant leap forward in 1915. On March 3 that year, the United States created NACA - the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics. NACA's groundbreaking research laid the foundation for its famous successor: NASA. From biplanes to rockets, NACA helped America reach for the stars!

story: Dive into the life of John Murray! Born in Coburg, Canada on March 3, 1841, this curious kid grew up to explore the ocean's mysteries. story: A future business titan took his first breath in Navsari, British India. Jamsetji Tata, born on March 3, 1839, would grow up to become a pioneering industrialist. title: Funk Bass Legend Rustee Allen Born
story: Rustee Allen, a funk and jazz fusion bass guitar master, entered the world in Monroe, Louisiana in 1951. Born on March 3, Allen would go on to make waves in the music scene. title: A Musical Journey Begins
story: Robyn Hitchcock's musical adventure started on March 3, 1952. Born in Paddington, London, he grew up to become a talented British rock and folk singer-songwriter.
